title: Test dataset for python-apd-restitution-analysis
author: Kok, Thomas
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The dataset consists of two files: 
 
 APD_res_min5.5_max13_step0.25_OFF_10_54.sif: optical map of a mouse heart which was injected with di-4-AN-PQ (!check). The mouse heart was paced from 5.5 to 13 Hz at 0.25 Hz steps, according to the APD restitution protocol. The recording was made with an Andor Solis camera. Image was captured by Callum Zgierski-Johnston 
 Basler_acA720-520um__40190569__20230919_114100243_vol_denoised.tif: optical map of a mouse heart which was injected with di-4-AN-PQ (!check). The recording consisted of multiple TIF files, captured by a Basler acA720-520um camera running at 478 fps. The software used for acquisition was the pylon viewer app by Basler. After collating the 2D TIF files into one large one, the volume was then denoised with  SUPPORT . The image was acquired by Thomas Kok. 
 
 This is the test dataset used for testing out the code from  https://github.com/tk231/python-apd-restitution-analysis  and acquired at the Institute for Experimental Cardiovascular Medicine (IEKM), University Medical Center Freiburg. 
 TODO: 
 
 Add animal experimentation license number!
